data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "The collapse resulted from emergent phase transition in eastern Mediterranean connectivity networks when centrality-dependent trade systems reached critical overload from simultaneous military innovation diffusion", "mechanism": "Iron metallurgy and naval warfare technologies (developed in periphery zones like Cyprus/Levant) diffused simultaneously ca. 1200 BCE, enabling non-palace actors—marauders, mercenaries, private traders—to bypass or overwhelm palace monopoly on force and long-distance exchange; network models predict phase transitions where hub-dependent architectures collapse suddenly when peripheral nodes gain edge-formation autonomy, explaining synchrony without common trigger, while Egypt survived as a 'decentralized network' with multiple military and economic hubs (Nile Delta, Thebes, Memphis) that could lose nodes without system-wide failure", "scope": {"covers": ["Synchrony without common cause", "Sea Peoples as symptom not root cause", "Technological diffusion as structural destabilizer", "Egyptian polycentricity"], "excludes": ["Climate as necessary component", "Single-site invasions as sufficient explanation", "Post-collapse Dark Age recovery dynamics"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Archaeological evidence shows iron weapons dominant in Sea Peoples assemblages and absent from Egyptian military equipment before 1150 BCE", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Cyprus and Levantine centers show uninterrupted palace continuity through 1200-1150 BCE despite technology diffusion", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Egyptian administrative documents reveal centralized single-node control of all military procurement and foreign trade under Ramesses III", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Network analysis of Ugarit tablets shows egalitarian peer-polity trade structure rather than hub-and-spoke palace centrality", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "Deliberately atypical: treats synchrony as emergent property of network topology rather than common external shock. Inverts standard climate determinism. Mechanism is institutional-technological co-evolution."}
